notes_md:
**không** means **“not”**. It’s the basic word to make a sentence negative.

Put **không** right before the verb or adjective you want to negate.  

For nouns, use **không phải** to say “is not” and **không có** to say “don’t have / there isn’t.”  
It’s the opposite of **có** (have/there is) and **phải** (must / correct).

Very flexible word — once you control this, you can say “no” to almost anything in Vietnamese.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He does not want to drink coffee.
She does not like watching movies.

translation.en: I do not understand the lesson.

example.en: I do not like to eat fish.

definitions.en: A word used to indicate negation or absence.
definitions.vi: Một từ được sử dụng để chỉ sự phủ định hoặc sự vắng mặt.
definitions.es: Una palabra utilizada para indicar negación o ausencia.

translation.en: Do you not want to go?

sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + không (not) + muốn (want) + uống (drink) + cà phê (coffee)
exampleSentences: Anh không muốn uống cà phê.
notes:

For nouns, use không phải to say “is not” and không có to say “don’t have / there isn’t.”  
It’s the opposite of có (have/there is) and phải (must / correct).

Very flexible word — once you control this, you can say “no” to almost anything in Vietnamese.
